calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Continued To May-03-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for default prove up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 2:25:36 p.m. and concluding at 3:13:12 p.m.

Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau Inc. appearing represented by Eric Jun, Esq.
Defendant Canada Coach Lines Group LTD not appearing.

BENJAMIN SEEMAN is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.
Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

BENJAMIN SEEMAN is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau Inc., and against Defendant Canada Coach Lines Group LTD as follows:

1. Damages: $30,690.00
2. Costs: $1,320.61
3. Attorney fees: $2,000.00

Court finds prejudgment intrest to be unconscionable.

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $34,010.61

Proposed judgment signed and filed. (D505)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Continued To Dec-28-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Continued To Aug-31-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
